steps.title: Use the first two years
steps.content: At $x=0$, the townhouse is worth $186{,}000$. A $3.5\%$ yearly increase means multiply by $1.035$, so after one year the value is $186{,}000(1.035)=192{,}510$.

steps.title: Find the exponential pattern
steps.content: In $y=a(b)^x$, $a$ is the starting value and $b$ is the multiplier for each year. Here $a=186{,}000$ and $b=1.035$, so the model is $y=186{,}000(1.035)^x$.

steps.title: Match the choice
steps.content: The choices with multipliers less than $1$ model decreases. The model with $1.35$ represents a $35\%$ increase, not a $3.5\%$ increase. The model $f(x)=186{,}000(1.035)^x$ uses the correct growth multiplier, so C is correct.
